executive_summary.bottom_line: Most thesis-disconfirming week of the trailing quarter. 30yr fell 16 bps to 6.30% on spot, but FOMC's hawkish hold compressed 2026 cut probability from ~18% to ~3%, and Warsh's confirmation hearing signaled intent to abandon forward guidance and the dot plot. Mechanical coiled spring unchanged at ~95% locked. The policy path to 5.5% lengthened materially. Three new shocks: (1) RE/MAX-Real Brokerage M&A drove RMAX +63%, but is idiosyncratic not Factor 1; (2) LGIH 45.6% Q1 cancel rate (all-time high) while peers' cancel rates fall — entry-level cohort fracturing; (3) admin/legislative pipeline accelerating with three 2026 EOs touching housing.
executive_summary.coiled_spring_state: No unlock-trigger threshold crossed. 95.1% locked (~48.3M). Nearest scenario at 6.25% releases 5.4M / 1.5M SAAR. Path arithmetic to 5.5% requires 80 bps drop. Fed-cut path closed (3% 2026 probability); MBS-spread compression path slow but possible.
executive_summary.whats_new: Two material new dimensions vs prior run: (a) admin/legislative pipeline expanded to ON-TAPE/IN-MOTION/SCUTTLE bucketing with 27 items including EO 14393 (Mar 13), EO Jan 20, $200B MBS directive (Jan 8), Housing for 21st Century Act (House passage Feb 9), and 10 SCUTTLE items including Section 121 expansion and assumable mortgage reform; (b) full housing value-chain map (12 layers) with sized flows and per-name vignettes.

binding_constraint_this_week.summary: Policy-path widening of MBS spreads / Fed communication architecture (not the spot 30yr).
binding_constraint_this_week.mechanism: Powell exits June; Warsh's stated intent to remove dot plot and forward guidance raises term premium on long Treasuries even at unchanged spot rates. MBS spreads stay 200-220 bps vs 160-180 bps historical.
binding_constraint_this_week.narrative_continuity: Six weeks ago the bottleneck was Treasury supply / term premium. Three weeks ago, MBS-Treasury spread. Today, Fed policy structure itself.

bottleneck_migration.next_likely: Credit-box width (Factor 4), if VantageScore 4.0 / FICO 10T loss-rate distributions print favorably summer 2026

No new SFR REIT 10-Q this week; INVH/AMH last filings remain Feb 2026; ~144k institutional held stock unchanged
NAR pending sales -1.1% YoY despite spring; FHFA HPI Mountain -1.1% MoM; LGIH 45.6% cancel = entry-level cohort breaking; FHA delinquency 5.23% Jan +166 bps from Sep
FHFA HPI +1.7% YoY (slowest in cycle); no fresh CPI shelter print

EO 14393 mortgage-credit access (Mar 13) + EO institutional-investor restriction (Jan 20) + Housing for 21st Century Act House passage (Feb 9) + FHFA/HUD VantageScore 4.0/FICO 10T (Apr 22) +; FOMC hawkish hold + Warsh forward-guidance removal posture -
Active listings 964k +49.6k vs 4w; permits -166k SAAR; supply rising existing, contracting forward-new

q1_factor_attribution.explanation: EHS gap = pre-2022 trend (~5.3M/yr) - current SAAR (3.98M) = ~1.32M units, ~25% deficit

factors.driver: Spot 30yr eased -16 bps; FOMC path widened MBS spread offset; mechanical share rose marginally
LGIH 45.6% cancel + Redfin 13.4% cancel + FHA delinq 5.23% all incremental
NAR pending -1.1% YoY ratifies JCHS down-revision direction; no fresh point estimate

q1_factor_attribution.read: 75% of gap explained by Factor 1 + Factor 5 (cyclical lock-in x affordability ceiling). Structural factors (2,3,4) real but smaller. WoW +2 pp net rotation toward Factors 1 and 5.

coiled_spring.path_arithmetic: 80 bps from spot to first trigger (5.50%); requires either 5+ Fed cuts (3% probability for 2026) or ~50 bps MBS-spread compression

homebuilder_cohort_pattern.thesis_read: The Diagonal: orders strong, closings weak, backlog inflating = affordability ceiling biting at conversion stage. LGIH cohort outlier localizes stress to FHA-leveraged entry-level. ASP bifurcation indicates luxury and entry-level cohorts decoupling structurally.

subsector_basket_dispersion.read: Strong outperformance; rates higher for longer trade
Flat; LDI drag offset by RKT/PFSI
Moved against thesis on the week

subsector_dispersion_synthesis: Rate-sensitive operating names (homebuilders, building products) sold off; rate-sensitive holding names (apartment REITs, SFR REITs) held up. Hawkish-rate regime, not coiled-spring-release regime. FOMC reset overpowered spot rate move.

correlation_thesis_validation.expected_behavior_this_week: Rate-down names (STC, FAF, RKT, homebuilders) should have outperformed on -16 bps move
correlation_thesis_validation.actual_behavior: They mostly didn't; homebuilders sold off -5% basket median
correlation_thesis_validation.read: FOMC reset overpowered spot rate move; this week's price action was about expected forward rates not spot rates - clean signal validating binding-constraint identification
correlation_thesis_validation.factor_exposure_validation: Apartment REIT short basket factor exposure correctly identified per short_baskets.yaml: Factor 1 (-0.40 weight, sign=negative) and Factor 5 (-0.25, sign=negative). Apartment REITs moved in short-thesis-against direction this week (+3% basket median on rate-up expectation reversal), confirming basket sensitivity correctly identified.

on_tape.item: EO Stopping Wall Street from Competing with Main Street Homebuyers
on_tape.mechanism: Treasury defines large institutional investor within 30 days; restricts federal financing for SFH bulk purchases; build-to-rent EXEMPTED; DOJ/FTC antitrust

on_tape.item: Housing for the 21st Century Act (HR 6644) - passed House 390-9
on_tape.mechanism: Bank public welfare investments cap 15->20%; de novo bank pilot; reciprocal deposits; longer exam cycle threshold $3B->$6B

on_tape.item: EO 14393 Promoting Access to Mortgage Credit
on_tape.mechanism: Directs CFPB/Fed/FDIC/OCC to: revise capital regs/risk weights for mortgages; modernize collateral/FHLB advances; replace TRID timing with materiality standard; exempt small-mortgage loans from QM points/fees caps; cure-first enforcement

on_tape.item: EO Removing Regulatory Barriers to Affordable Home Construction
on_tape.mechanism: EPA/HUD/CEQ/FHFA review; chattel lending for manufactured housing; low-balance home mortgages

on_tape.item: FHFA + HUD authorize VantageScore 4.0 and FICO 10T
on_tape.mechanism: Credit-scoring competition; rent-payment history scoring expands ~4-7M consumers

on_tape.item: OBBBA tax provisions (MID $750K permanent; PMI deductible 2026; SALT $40K through 2029)

in_motion.item: Senate version of institutional-investor bill - sent to House
in_motion.mechanism: Restricts large institutional SFH purchases; complements Jan 20 EO

in_motion.item: Senate ROAD to Housing Act - Senate version of HR 6644
in_motion.mechanism: Mirrors House Housing for 21st Century Act

in_motion.item: First-Time Homebuyer Tax Credit - referred to House Ways and Means
HR Kean Housing Affordability Tax Credit Act - $10K credit for FTHB
Trump paving way to reduce GSE capital requirements
in_motion.mechanism: Reduces GSE capital -> MBS spread compression

in_motion.item: House FS markup: HR 941 LENDER Act, HR 8290 Exchange Rate Accountability
in_motion.mechanism: Small lender support; cross-border financial flows

in_motion.item: House FS Housing Subcommittee hearing: Reinsurance & Credit Risk Transfers
Senate Banking: Warsh Fed Chair nomination hearing

scuttle.mechanism: Walks back Nov 2025 IPO posture; GSE conservatorship status indefinite
$250K -> $510K single; $500K -> $1.02M joint; never indexed since 1997; direct lock-in attack

scuttle.mechanism: Assumable rate transfer = direct sub-4% release without rate change; direct catalyst

Subsidizes Year 1-2 mortgage payment for FTHB

scuttle.item: OCC / FDIC / Fed capital relief for mortgage origination
scuttle.mechanism: Reduces capital cost of mortgage assets; supports MBS-Treasury spread compression
Expands supply of affordable rental units

Conforming loan limit beyond formula increase

three_risks.risk: Inflation re-acceleration through Sun Belt rent + tariff-driven goods
three_risks.falsifiable_threshold: US CPI shelter MoM > +0.4% for two consecutive months (May 12 + June 11 prints) AND core goods CPI > +0.3% MoM through Q2 2026
three_risks.mechanism: Sun Belt apartment rent re-acceleration pulls core CPI shelter back up; tariff-driven goods compounds; Fed's hold becomes hike scenario by Q3, materially extending lock-in
three_risks.watch_trigger: Apartment REIT Q1 same-store rent prints (MAA, CPT, IRT - week of May 12-16). If MAA and CPT report rent growth > +1% YoY (vs -1 to flat in late 2025), Sun Belt re-acceleration is firing. Compound with Iran-driven oil >$80 -> core PCE doesn't fall to 2% in 2026 -> Fed extends hold or hikes.
three_risks.thesis_impact: Catastrophic on Factor 1; spot 30yr re-rises to 6.75-7.0%; MBS spreads stay wide; Tier-1 builder Q2-Q3 cohort takes operational hits; UWMC insider supply weighs on originator basket

three_risks.risk: Warsh delivers on removing forward guidance / dot plot before MBS spreads compress
three_risks.falsifiable_threshold: First post-Warsh-confirmation FOMC press conference (likely July 30, 2026) explicitly removes dot plot and forward guidance from SEP - independent of cut path
three_risks.mechanism: Removing forward guidance raises term premium on long Treasuries even at unchanged spot rates; MBS spreads stay 200-220 bps; 30yr stays >6%; Factor 1 unlock catalyst pushed out by 6-12 months independent of core PCE
three_risks.watch_trigger: Warsh transition (June 2026); first SEP under his chairmanship (July 30 FOMC); any pre-meeting WSJ trial balloon on dot plot removal
three_risks.thesis_impact: Material on Factor 1; long-duration thesis names (originators, title) face multiple compression on path uncertainty; apartment REITs continue to outperform

three_risks.risk: FHA delinquency cycle deepens; entry-level cohort lockout becomes structural
three_risks.falsifiable_threshold: FHA serious delinquency (90+ days) > 6.0% by July 2026 (currently 5.23% Jan; trajectory implies 5.8% by mid-year if linear). LGIH and DHI Q2 cancel rates exceed 50% / 20% respectively.
three_risks.mechanism: Even if Factor 1 (rates) eases, FHA-leveraged cohort has structurally lower qualification capacity due to inflation-driven income-debt-ratio compression + FHA MIP cost; unlock catalyst arrives but absorbing demand structurally short - Factor 4 binding becomes active constraint
three_risks.watch_trigger: HUD FHA Single-Family Loan Performance Trends report (monthly cadence). LGIH Q2 print ~late July. DHI Q3 print ~late July.
three_risks.thesis_impact: Modest on broad thesis; substantial on LGIH-style entry-level builder cohort; cohort bifurcation becomes structural; relief from rates falling doesn't reach marginal entry-level buyer; apartment REIT short thesis weakens further (involuntary renters become structural not cyclical)

underappreciated_catalyst.summary: VantageScore 4.0 + FICO 10T historical loss-rate distributions, due summer 2026
underappreciated_catalyst.time_to_tape: 60-120 days (loss-rate publication summer 2026; lender adoption signaling 2026 H2-2027 H1)
underappreciated_catalyst.consensus_missing: Mainstream credit-scoring coverage focuses on headline competition between VantageScore and FICO; read-through to housing demand treated as ancillary. Bloomberg / WSJ frame it as fee-and-vendor story. Neither connects rent-history scoring to FHA-cohort demand recovery.
underappreciated_catalyst.contrarian_read: Three pieces of evidence consensus is not connecting: (1) VantageScore 4.0 incorporates rent-payment history -> expands qualifiable-borrower pool by ~4-7M consumers; (2) Pulte's 'stay tuned' on LLPAs = GSEs reserving option to price new-cohort loans differently; (3) Historical FICO 10T data publishes summer 2026 - if loss-rate distributions show parity with FICO Classic, lenders adopt because LLPA pricing accretive.
underappreciated_catalyst.specific_catalyst_data: Per Federal Reserve Survey of Consumer Finances (2022), ~26M renting households whose median income materially above what their reported FICO scores would qualify them for. Even 15-20% absorbed via rent-history scoring shifts Factor 4 ceiling math materially.
underappreciated_catalyst.trade_relevant_insight: This catalyst fires before the Factor 1 unlock. If summer 2026 loss-rate data is favorable, LGIH-cohort affordability stress eases via demand side (more qualified buyers) rather than supply side (lower rates). Entry-level builders DHI/LGIH/KBH set up for non-rate-driven catalyst consensus is ignoring.

macro_chain_check_q7.synthesis: Chain operating coherently; disconfirmation in how constrained the unlock path is, not in any link breaking. Watch: Iran-Israel ceasefire credibility, Treasury issuance composition, Fed MBS portfolio runoff trajectory.

what_would_change_my_mind: 30yr crosses below 6.0% sustained 5+ trading days -> material Factor 1 update
MBS-Treasury spread tightens to <180 bps -> independent of Fed cut, would deliver 30yr drop without Fed action
Warsh-related WSJ / Bloomberg trial balloon explicitly walks back forward-guidance removal -> removes Risk 2
Apartment REIT Q1 same-store rent prints (MAA, CPT, IRT) reaccelerate above +1% YoY -> Sun Belt thesis weakens
LGIH or KBH Q2 cancel rate <30% -> entry-level cohort stress easing
FHFA implements EO 14393 small-mortgage QM exemption rule -> direct policy catalyst on credit box

data_integrity_flags.description: Existing Home Sales SAAR shows 3,980k with days_stale=61 - Script 01 known cadence-aware delta issue
Median Home Price shows $405,300 with days_stale=212 - quarterly Census cadence; OK as-is but flagged
New Home Sales days_stale=120 - quarterly cadence; OK
Some homebuilder ASP figures may have unit truncation: MHO $459 and MTH $373 in raw data are clearly thousands; per Betsy 2026-05-01 commit b97f75a fix may not have flowed yet to all consumers
REIT period_end_date is null on several names; uses fiscal_year_end fallback per data/sec_reit_homes.csv 2026-05-01 fix
MAA home count 5,995 in 10-K parse appears partial; cross-reference NAREIT before relying
Apollo Slok deck (FILE_4211.pdf) referenced for empirical anchors; specific slide reads sourced from prior reading per docs/reports/README.md

what_we_got_wrong_last_week: Inaugural deep-dive run; prior weekly was 31KB skeleton (Run 2026-05-01T16:30Z, framework SHA 3bc1b7f). No out-of-sample forecasts in prior run yet falsified. From next week onward, this section will track explicit forecast deltas. New additions in this run vs prior: confidence column on factor scorecard; Q1 attribution decomposition; three falsifiable risks (Warsh-specific risk new); time-to-tape tag on catalyst; full ON-TAPE/IN-MOTION/SCUTTLE pipeline (27 items vs prior 7); 12-layer value-chain map; bottleneck-migration narrative.
